---
title: "accident-analysis"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
date: "2024-05-07"
---

#Read the data
```{r}
Crash <- read.csv("/Users/ningzhu/Downloads/Crash_Reporting_-_Drivers_Data.csv")
```

#Cleaning data part
```{r}
Crash <- subset(Crash, Vehicle.Year %in% 1900:2024)
Crash$Crash.Date.Time <- as.POSIXct(Crash$Crash.Date.Time, format = "%m/%d/%Y %I:%M:%S %p")
# Extract year from Crash.Date.Time and assign it to Crash_year
Crash$Crash_year <- as.numeric(format(Crash$Crash.Date.Time, "%Y"))
Crash$Vehicle.Year <- as.numeric(Crash$Vehicle.Year)
Crash$Used.year <- Crash$Crash_year - Crash$Vehicle.Year
Crash
```






```{r}
#There are 1415 in "SUSPECTED SERIOUS INJURY"
#There are 11870 in "SUSPECTED MINOR INJURY"
#There are 153 in "FATAL INJURY"
Crash$Injury.Category <- ifelse(Crash$Injury.Severity == "SUSPECTED SERIOUS INJURY" | 
                                Crash$Injury.Severity == "FATAL INJURY", 1,
                                ifelse(Crash$Injury.Severity == "SUSPECTED MINOR INJURY", 0, NA))
Data <- Crash[Crash$Injury.Severity == "SUSPECTED SERIOUS INJURY" |
              Crash$Injury.Severity == "SUSPECTED MINOR INJURY" |
              Crash$Injury.Severity == "FATAL INJURY", ]
new_Data <- Data[, c("Used.year","Driver.At.Fault", "Speed.Limit", "Injury.Category")]
new_Data <- subset(new_Data, Driver.At.Fault != "Unknown")
new_Data$Driver.At.Fault[new_Data$Driver.At.Fault == "Yes"] <- 1
new_Data$Driver.At.Fault[new_Data$Driver.At.Fault == "No"] <- 0
new_Data
```

```{r}
negative_values <- new_Data$Used.year < 0
new_Data[negative_values, ]
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Assuming your dataset is named new_Data
new_Data <- new_Data %>%
  filter(Used.year >= 0)
new_Data
```





#Virtualization_part
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

totals_by_category <- new_Data %>%
  group_by(Injury.Category) %>%
  summarise(Total = n(), .groups = 'drop')

speed_injury_data <- new_Data %>%
  group_by(Speed.Limit, Injury.Category) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  left_join(totals_by_category, by = "Injury.Category") %>%
  mutate(Percentage = (Count / Total) * 100) 

ggplot(speed_injury_data, aes(x = as.factor(Speed.Limit), y = Percentage, fill = Injury.Category)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") + 
  ggtitle("Percentage of Incidents by Speed Limit and Injury Category") +
  xlab("Speed Limit (mph)") +
  ylab("Percentage of Incidents") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1))  # Rotate x-axis labels for better readability

```


```{r}
# Create mosaic plot
library(ggplot2)
mosaicplot(table(new_Data$Injury.Category, new_Data$Driver.At.Fault),
           main = "Mosaic Plot of Injury Category vs. Driver.At.Fault",
           color = c("lightblue", "pink"),
           xlab = "Injury Category",
           ylab = "Driver's Fault")


```



```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Ensure that Injury.Category is a factor with meaningful level names
new_Data$Injury.Category <- factor(new_Data$Injury.Category, levels = c(0, 1),
                                   labels = c("Minor Injury", "Serious/Fatal Injury"))

# Create the boxplot
ggplot(new_Data, aes(x = Injury.Category, y = Used.year, fill = Injury.Category)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1", labels = c("Minor Injury", "Serious/Fatal Injury")) +
  ggtitle("Used Years by Injury Category") +
  xlab("Injury Category") +
  ylab("Used Year") +
  theme_minimal()  # Using a minimal theme for a cleaner look

```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Calculate the IQR and define bounds for outliers within each injury category
cleaned_Data <- new_Data %>%
  group_by(Injury.Category) %>%
  filter(between(Used.year, quantile(Used.year, 0.25) - 1.5 * IQR(Used.year), quantile(Used.year, 0.75) + 1.5 * IQR(Used.year))) %>%
  ungroup()


# Create the boxplot with the cleaned data
ggplot(cleaned_Data, aes(x = Injury.Category, y = Used.year, fill = Injury.Category)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  ggtitle("Used year by Injury Category (Outliers Removed)") +
  xlab("Injury Category") +
  ylab("Used year") +
  theme_minimal()  # Using a minimal theme for a cleaner look


```

# Model_Part
# Cross Validation: We separate the data to training and test.
# Logistic model
```{r}
set.seed(1)
train_indices <- sample(1:nrow(new_Data), nrow(new_Data) / 2)
train_data <- new_Data[train_indices,]
test_data <- new_Data[-train_indices,]
```



```{r}
#Find P-value
glm.fit <- glm(Injury.Category ~ Used.year + Driver.At.Fault + Speed.Limit, data = train_data, family = binomial)
summary(glm.fit)
```


# Predict of glm

```{r}
# Assuming train_data is available and properly formatted
# Load necessary library
library(boot)

# Fit the logistic regression model
glm.fit <- glm(Injury.Category ~ Used.year + Driver.At.Fault + Speed.Limit, 
               data = train_data, 
               family = binomial)

# Define a function to perform cross-validation
# The function needs to be defined to work with the cv.glm() function
cv.glm.fun <- function(data, indices) {
  # Train the model on a subset of data defined by indices
  fit <- glm(Injury.Category ~ Used.year + Driver.At.Fault + Speed.Limit,
             data = data[indices, ], family = binomial)
  
  # Return the fitted model
  return(fit)
}

# Perform 10-fold cross-validation
set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
cv.result <- cv.glm(train_data, glm.fit, K = 10, cost = function(r, pi = 0) mean(abs(r-pi) > 0.5))

# Print the results
# This will show the cross-validated estimate of the prediction error
print(cv.result$delta[1])  # The first element is typically the raw cross-validation estimate

```



```{r}
library(MASS)
library(caret)

set.seed(1234)

K <- 10  # Example: 50-fold cross-validation
error_rates <- numeric(K)
accuracies <- numeric(K)

for (fold in 1:K) {
  fold_indices <- sample(1:nrow(new_Data))
  fold_size <- floor(nrow(new_Data) / K)
  validation_indices <- fold_indices[((fold - 1) * fold_size + 1):(fold * fold_size)]
  train_indices <- fold_indices[setdiff(1:nrow(new_Data), validation_indices)]
  train_data <- new_Data[train_indices, ]
  test_data <- new_Data[validation_indices, ]
  glm.fit <- glm(Injury.Category ~ Used.year + Driver.At.Fault + Speed.Limit,
                 data = train_data,
                 family = binomial)
  
  y_pred_probs <- predict(glm.fit, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
  y_pred <- ifelse(y_pred_probs > 0.5, 1, 0)
  error_rates[fold] <- mean(y_pred != test_data$Injury.Category)
}

avg_error_rate <- mean(error_rates)
avg_error_rate
cm <- confusionMatrix(as.factor(y_pred), as.factor(test_data$Injury.Category))
accuracies <- cm$overall['Accuracy']
cm
accuracies
```


```{r}
library(MASS)
n <- nrow(new_Data)
res <- 0
for (k in 1:50) {
  glm.fit <- glm(Injury.Category ~ Used.year + Driver.At.Fault + Speed.Limit,
            data = train_data,
            family = binomial)
  y_pred <- predict(glm.fit, test_data)
  predicted_labels <- ifelse(y_pred > 0, 1, 0)
  res <- res + mean(predicted_labels != test_data$Injury.Category)
  }
res <- res / 50
res
```

```{r}
library(boot)
k <- 10
n <- nrow(new_Data)

set.seed(123)
shuffled_indices <- sample(1:n)

# Function to perform k-fold cross-validation manually
cv_error_rates <- numeric(k)

for (i in 1:k) {
  test_indices <- ((n/k)*(i-1) + 1):((n/k)*i)
  train_indices <- setdiff(1:n, test_indices)
  train_data <- new_Data[shuffled_indices[train_indices], ]
  test_data <- new_Data[shuffled_indices[test_indices], ]
  glm.fit <- glm(Injury.Category ~ Used.year + Driver.At.Fault + Speed.Limit, 
                 data = train_data, family = binomial())
  y_pred <- predict(glm.fit, test_data, type = "response")
  predicted_labels <- ifelse(y_pred > 0.5, 1, 0)
  cv_error_rates[i] <- mean(predicted_labels != test_data$Injury.Category)
}

average_cv_error_rate <- mean(cv_error_rates)
print(average_cv_error_rate)

```




```{r}
library(caret)
conf_matrix <- confusionMatrix(as.factor(predicted_labels), as.factor(test_data$Injury.Category))
conf_matrix$table
conf_matrix$overall['Accuracy']
```


```{r}
library(MASS)
n <- nrow(new_Data)
res <- 0
for (k in 1:10) {
  lda.fit <- lda(Injury.Category ~ Used.year + Driver.At.Fault + Speed.Limit, 
                 data = train_data)
  y_pred <- predict(lda.fit, test_data)$class
  res <- res + mean(y_pred != test_data$Injury.Category)
}
res <- res / 10
res
```

```{r}
pred <- predict(lda.fit, test_data)$class
confusion_mat <- table(test_data$Injury.Category, pred, dnn = c("True", "Predicted"))
confusion_mat 
#Calculate the accuracy
accuracy <- sum(diag(confusion_mat))/sum(confusion_mat)
accuracy
```


```{r}
library(MASS)
n <- nrow(new_Data)
res <- 0
for (k in 1:50) {
  qda.fit <- qda(Injury.Category ~ Used.year + Driver.At.Fault + Speed.Limit, data = train_data)
  y_pred <- predict(qda.fit, test_data)$class
  res <- res + mean(y_pred != test_data$Injury.Category)
}
res <- res / 50
res
```


```{r}
pred <- predict(qda.fit, test_data)$class
confusion_mat <- table(test_data$Injury.Category, pred, dnn = c("True", "Predicted"))
confusion_mat 
#Calculate the accuracy
accuracy <- sum(diag(confusion_mat))/sum(confusion_mat)
accuracy
```



```{r}
library(MASS)
lda_fit <- lda(Injury.Category ~ Used.year + Driver.At.Fault + Speed.Limit, data = train_data)
lda_fit
qda_model <- qda(Injury.Category ~ Used.year + Driver.At.Fault + Speed.Limit, data = train_data)
qda_model
```

```{r}

```


```{r}
library(MASS)  # for lda
library(caret)  # for creating folds and possibly for accuracy calculations

# Assume train_data is your full dataset you want to perform CV on
set.seed(123)  # for reproducibility

# Define the number of folds
K <- 50  # 10-fold cross-validation

# Create indices for the k-folds
folds <- createFolds(train_data$Injury.Category, k = K, list = TRUE, returnTrain = TRUE)

# Initialize accumulator for average error rate
total_error_rate <- 0

# Perform K-fold cross-validation
for (i in seq_along(folds)) {
  # Split data into training and testing based on folds
  train_indices <- folds[[i]]
  test_indices <- setdiff(1:nrow(train_data), train_indices)
  
  train_subset <- train_data[train_indices, ]
  test_subset <- train_data[test_indices, ]
  
  # Fit LDA model on the training subset
  lda.fit <- lda(Injury.Category ~ Used.year + Driver.At.Fault + Speed.Limit, data = train_subset)
  
  # Predict on the testing subset
  predictions <- predict(lda.fit, test_subset)
  y_pred <- predictions$class
  
  # Calculate error rate for this fold
  fold_error_rate <- mean(y_pred != test_subset$Injury.Category)
  total_error_rate <- total_error_rate + fold_error_rate
}

average_error_rate <- total_error_rate / K
average_error_rate
```
